capability_objective: Status audit only: locate Recycled packing tape in Warehouse C and A4 printer paper in Warehouse D. The two lookups are independent, so search for them in the same assistant action. After each search returns one unique ID, retrieve both records together and compare their returned statuses. Do not create, update, cancel, or notify anyone. If either search has zero or multiple exact matches, stop before dependent calls and tell me which lookup is ambiguous.
prompt:
Perform a status-only warehouse inventory audit in the provided sandbox. Read README.md first and use the genuine inventory registry executable it documents through Pi's Bash tool. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing data, generated runtime data, or audit evidence, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under .harness.

Locate Recycled packing tape in Warehouse C and A4 printer paper in Warehouse D by exact item name and warehouse. Ground every reported record field by first searching the registry and then retrieving the complete current record by the unique stable ID returned by that item's search. The searches are independent: make them as exactly two sibling Bash calls in one assistant action, with one registry operation per call. Do not combine them in one shell call and do not launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both results. Only after both searches have each returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, make the two complete-record retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash calls in the next assistant action that uses the registry, again with one registry operation per call and only the IDs returned by the corresponding searches. If either search has zero or multiple exact matches or lacks a stable ID, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved lookup, and do not create the report. Compare only status fields returned by the complete records, never search summaries.

This is a read-only audit. Do not create, update, cancel, or notify anyone, and do not invoke any registry operation except the two required searches and, when both lookups resolve, the two required retrievals. Do not touch similarly named, related, or archived items. Do not alter the registry, its backing or runtime data, its audit trail, or protected verification files, and do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit events. Leave no scratch artifacts.

When both records are retrieved, write status_audit.md as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated plain-text sentences, one per line, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, stable IDs, quantities, bin details, descriptions, interpretations, or recommendations. Put Recycled packing tape first and A4 printer paper second. Format each record line exactly as `<name> in <warehouse> has status <status>.`, using only fields from that complete record and preserving returned values exactly. If any of those three fields is absent from its complete record, substitute the literal word `unknown` for that field rather than inferring it from the request, a search summary, or another source. Make the third line exactly `The returned statuses match.` or `The returned statuses differ.`, as appropriate; if either complete record lacks a status field, make it exactly `The returned status comparison is unknown.` Return exactly the file content as the final response.
